steps.title: Use the generalization caution
steps.content: The researchers studied respondents from Serbia, which the passage identifies as having a developing economy. Their caution is about applying findings from one economic context to customers generally.

steps.title: Address the sample limitation
steps.content: Choice B speculates about wealthier countries, Choice C shifts to Serbian sample size, and Choice D turns the finding into retailer advice. The remaining choice calls for research across levels of economic development. The answer is A.
